title: The Pentagon Stops a Troubled GPS Control System
summary: The Pentagon has canceled a GPS control system called OCX because it had too many problems. This system was supposed to help control GPS satellites, which help people find locations. It was started 16 years ago and cost billions of dollars, but it kept having issues and delays. The company RTX worked on it, but tests showed it wasn't ready. Instead, the Space Force will keep using and improving the old control system. This decision means the military won't use OCX to get new GPS upgrades like better signals. The cancellation happened after spending over $6 billion. The Space Force wants to focus on faster and better ways to get things done.

background_read: GPS stands for Global Positioning System. It uses satellites in space to help people and the military know where they are. The Pentagon is part of the U.S. government that makes decisions about the military. The Space Force is a newer branch that handles space missions. OCX was a project to make a better control system for GPS, but it had many delays and issues, so it was stopped. Now, the military will improve the old system instead.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Pentagon and Space Force, with RTX as the contractor.
WHAT: Canceled the OCX GPS control system due to problems.
WHERE: In the United States, for military and civilian GPS use.
WHY: Because OCX had too many issues and wasn't ready, so they'll use the old system instead.

perspectives.description: The Space Force canceled OCX to avoid risks and focus on improving the current system.
RTX delivered the system but supports the government's decision on next steps.
